intro:
I couldn't find any information on Olgasis gauntlettii. However, based on the name, it seems to be a misspelling of Olax sessilifolia's related plant, Olax gaillontii seems to be misspelled too however I was able to find information on Olax ( genus ) of the family Olacaceae. 

Olax, commonly known as the soursop or guanabana, is a genus of flowering plants that provide a variety of health benefits. The health benefits of Olax include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and prevent infections. The bioactive constituents of Olax include saponins, alkaloids, and flavonoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Olax include teas, infusions, and capsules, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including digestive issues, fever, and skin problems.
